rulings.Case Title: LEROY CABRAL VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default Against Foley Electric Company
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on the Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 28, 2017, line 1. DEFENDANT FOLEY ELECTRIC, INC'S Motion To Set Aside Default. Defendant Foley Electric, Inc.'s motion to set aside default against Foley Electric Company is off calendar at the moving party's request. =(503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: LESLIE STROTHERS VS. LAMONS GASKET COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 28, 2017 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant The Goodyear Tire & Rubber Company's motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to Issue 1 (punitive damages) and granted as to Issue 2 (asbestosis). As to summary judgment, defendant sustained its initial burden pursuant to the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Plaintiff's deposition testimony in this action, plaintiff's declaration, defendant's responses to standard interrogatories (Exhibit Q), and Ernest DeMarse's deposition testimony (Exhibit P) create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (Goodyearite sheet gasket material) attributable to defendant. As to summary adjudication of plaintiff's claim for punitive damages, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Plaintiff's September 1, 2017 supplemental responses to interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. As to summary adjudication of plaintiff's claims related to asbestosis, the motion is granted to the extent that plaintiff is barred from seeking damages related to asbestosis. This ruling shall not be considered a determination whether plaintiff may introduce evidence regarding an asbestosis diagnosis at trial. Plaintiff's objections are sustained as to defendant's Exhibit L and overruled as to Exhibit M. Defendant's objections are overruled as to plaintiff's Exhibits A, H, I, J, K, L, M, P, Q and sustained as to Exhibits N, O, R. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)
